behavior_analysis.behavior: each drawer slides out for storage access to its internal volume
behavior_analysis.reason: All six drawer components (`drawer_row1_col1` through `drawer_row3_col2`) are present, each with an associated handle component, enabling the interaction necessary for sliding and accessing internal volume. The roles are correctly assigned.

drawer_row3_col2.computation_notes: Largest bottom drawer with maximum damping coefficient for smooth operation under full load.

drawer_row2_col1.computation_notes: Medium drawer with standard sliding mechanism. Damping slightly higher due to increased depth.

drawer_row1_col2.computation_notes: Larger drawer requires heavier-duty slides with increased damping coefficient. Pin diameter scaled for higher load capacity.

drawer_row1_col1.computation_notes: Sliding drawer mechanism with ball-bearing or roller slides. Damping based on typical soft-close drawer slides. No rotational torque required.

drawer_row3_col1.computation_notes: Bottom drawer with increased depth requires slightly higher damping and latch force.

drawer_row2_col2.computation_notes: Similar sized drawer to row2_col1 with proportionally scaled hardware dimensions.
